# copilot-instructions-template

> Lean/Kaizen GitHub Copilot template for VS Code projects. It scaffolds model-pinned agents, reusable skills, hooks, MCP config, workspace identity files, and update protocols into a user's repository.

## Start here

- [.github/copilot-instructions.md](.github/copilot-instructions.md): Main always-loaded instruction file. Core methodology and guardrails.
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): Machine entry point. Trigger phrases, setup, update, and restore sequences.
- [SETUP.md](SETUP.md): Remote bootstrap protocol for consumer projects.
- [UPDATE.md](UPDATE.md): Remote update and restore protocol.
- [MIGRATION.md](MIGRATION.md): Per-version migration registry.

## Model strategy

| Surface | Recommended model | Role |
|---------|-------------------|------|
| `setup.agent.md` | Claude Sonnet 4.6 | Interactive setup and onboarding |
| `coding.agent.md` | GPT-5.3-Codex | Implementation, refactoring, multi-step coding |
| `review.agent.md` | GPT-5.4 | Deep review, debugging, architectural analysis |
| `fast.agent.md` | Claude Haiku 4.5 | Quick lookups and lightweight edits |
| `update.agent.md` | Claude Sonnet 4.6 | Upstream instruction updates |
| `doctor.agent.md` | Claude Sonnet 4.6 | Read-only health checks |

## Skills

The repo ships 13 skills in `.github/skills/` and `template/skills/`. High-signal examples:

- `extension-review`: audit VS Code extensions against detected stack signals
- `test-coverage-review`: audit test coverage and recommend local tests plus CI workflows
- `tool-protocol`: search before building automation
- `skill-management`: discover and manage skills on demand
- `mcp-management`: configure external tool access
- `plugin-management`: discover and manage agent plugins

Canonical inventory for all skills, guides, and agents lives in `.copilot/workspace/DOC_INDEX.json`.

## Validation

- Main suite: `bash tests/test-hooks.sh && bash tests/test-guard-destructive.sh && bash tests/test-sync-version.sh && bash tests/test-security-edge-cases.sh`
- Docs drift: `bash tests/test-doc-consistency.sh`
- Generated metadata: `bash scripts/sync-doc-index.sh --check && bash scripts/sync-llms-context.sh --check`

## Optional

- [llms-ctx.txt](llms-ctx.txt): generated compact LLM context pack
- [llms-ctx-full.txt](llms-ctx-full.txt): generated expanded LLM context pack
- [docs/](docs/): detailed human-readable guides
